contentSections.heading: What is a free lyric video maker?
contentSections.intro: A free lyric video maker turns your song audio into a complete lyric video without forcing you to build a timeline first. Upload an audio file, let AI transcribe and sync the lyrics, review the lines, choose a visual direction, preview the result, and export an MP4 in the format your release needs.
bullets: Input is your song audio, not a pasted link or a video clip.
It can create a finished lyric video with editable lyrics, beat sync, AI scene imagery, and preview.
Output is a platform-ready MP4 in widescreen, vertical, or square format.
The difference from free template editors is that lyric transcription and timing are done for you.

contentSections.heading: free lyric video maker specs at a glance

rows: Accepted input
Audio upload only: MP3, WAV, FLAC, AAC, OGG, or M4A, up to 100MB per file.
Lyrics
AI transcribes vocals automatically in 50+ languages, then lets you edit every line before export.
Sync
Beat-aware line timing keeps lyrics readable and musical instead of simply dropping captions on a fixed grid.
Visuals
Storyboard planning and AI scene-image generation create backgrounds around the mood of the song.
Exports
MP4 with original audio in 16:9, 9:16, or 1:1 for YouTube, Shorts, TikTok, Reels, and feeds.
Start
Free credits are available at the start, so you can preview the workflow before spending more time on a release.

contentSections.heading: Free maker trade-offs to check before you start
contentSections.intro: Free tools are not all free in the same way. For a release asset, the practical question is whether the free path gives you enough control to make something you would actually publish.

rows: Typing lyrics
A useful free maker should transcribe from the audio, because manual typing is where most projects stall.
Preview depth
You need to watch the whole video, not only a teaser, before deciding whether the style fits the song.
Format choice
A release usually needs at least a YouTube version and a vertical cut, so 16:9 and 9:16 both matter.
Edit access
Free should still let you correct misheard words and awkward line breaks before rendering.
Upgrade pressure
The best free workflow lets the result prove itself before asking you to spend more time or credits.
Reuse
If you like the style, you should be able to repeat it across the next single without rebuilding from zero.

contentSections.heading: Best practices for making a free lyric video look intentional
contentSections.intro: Use these checks before you render, especially when the same project needs more than one platform format.
bullets: Start with the cleanest audio master you have; better vocal clarity means fewer lyric corrections later.
Preview the video on a phone before export, because most readability problems only show up on a small screen.
Use one strong style rather than stacking every effect; restraint makes a free video feel more professional.
Fix line breaks where a phrase naturally breathes instead of accepting every automatic split.
Export both 16:9 and 9:16 while the project is fresh, so release-week clips match the full video.
Keep a simple naming habit for projects, such as artist, song, aspect ratio, and date.
Save your preferred font and color choices as a style reference for the next track.

contentSections.heading: Why free is most useful at the first-draft stage
contentSections.intro: A free lyric video maker turns your song audio into a complete lyric video without building a timeline first. The choices that matter happen before you render — here are the principles worth keeping.

cards.title: Free is most valuable for the first draft
cards.description: Free credits with no card and no trial countdown let you preview the whole video, switch styles, and fix a misheard word before you commit — the first version is genuinely yours to export.

cards.title: Lyrics are structure, not decoration
cards.description: The words are the structure the viewer follows, not a caption added last. Always transcribe first, align to the beat second, and design the scene third.

cards.title: AI does the heavy lifting, you keep the taste
cards.description: AI handles transcription, timing, and scene generation. Whether the style fits, a line break should change, or you need a vertical, square, or widescreen export stays your call.

cards.title: The scene serves the lyric
cards.description: Viewers should never choose between reading the line and watching the scene. Keep contrast strong, lines short, and motion restrained so the visuals support the words.

cards.title: Review in three passes
cards.description: Watch once for words to catch transcription errors, once for rhythm to catch lines that arrive early or late, and once for platform fit so it still reads in a phone feed.

cards.title: One source, every format
cards.description: A single project becomes a 16:9 full version, a 9:16 vertical, and a 1:1 square without retyping lyrics or rebuilding timing — which is what makes a release feel planned.

cards.title: Build it while the song is fresh
cards.description: Make the lyric video while the song is still in your ear: you will hear awkward line breaks faster, catch misheard words sooner, and judge where the energy should rise or stay quiet.

contentSections.heading: free lyric video maker checklist before export
contentSections.intro: A quick pre-export review catches most quality problems while they are still cheap to fix.

rows: Audio readiness
Accepted formats are MP3, WAV, FLAC, AAC, OGG, and M4A; keep the file under 100MB.
Language review
The system auto-detects 50+ languages, but bilingual hooks and names deserve a manual pass.
Timing review
Beat sync gives a strong baseline; nudge any line that lands early or late against the vocal.
Scene review
Generated images should match the mood without making text hard to read.
Export review
Check 16:9, 9:16, and 1:1 separately because readable text size changes by format.

items.question: Is this lyric video creator really free?
items.answer: Yes. The free plan lets you make lyric videos, preview the full result, and export — no credit card required, no subscription needed.

items.question: Do I need to type or paste the lyrics myself?
items.answer: No — lyrics are transcribed automatically from the audio. The AI lyric video generator handles transcription in 50+ languages. You can paste your own lyrics file instead if you prefer.

items.question: Can I make vertical lyric videos for TikTok on the free plan?
items.answer: Yes. Every free video can export in 16:9, 9:16, and 1:1 — one click each.

items.question: Can I use free videos commercially?
items.answer: Yes — even on the free plan, your exports are yours to monetize, ad-supported channels included. Just make sure you have the rights to the music you upload.

items.question: What does a subscription add?
items.answer: More credits, all styles including Pro exclusives, and higher priority processing. The free plan already covers making and exporting your lyric video.

items.question: What file formats can I upload?
items.answer: MP3, WAV, FLAC, AAC, and OGG — up to 15 minutes. Add lyrics to video automatically online free from any of these formats.

items.question: Is this a free lyric video generator or a free lyric video maker?
items.answer: Both. LyricVideoMaker is a free AI lyric video maker — or a free AI lyrics video maker — and a free AI lyric video generator in one. Call it a free lyric video generator or a free lyrics video generator: the flow is identical — upload a song, get a synced lyric video, and export free.

items.question: What happens when I use up my free credits?
items.answer: Nothing breaks — the videos you already made stay yours to keep and re-export. When you want more, a paid plan adds credits and all styles, but the free plan already lets you make, preview, and export a full lyric video.
